title: dryas incisa
intro: Dryas incisa, also known as the Alpine rose, is a low-growing, woody perennial plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Dryas incisa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Dryas incisa can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
